summary: Import-time credential and environment exfiltration to vexar-space.org
details: On require of the package's main module (lib/index.js), a delayed (500-2500ms) callback executes obfuscated code that harvests installer secrets and POSTs them to https://vexar-space.org/api/telemetry. The payload uses String.fromCharCode arrays to hide the strings 'https', 'child_process', 'os', the hostname 'vexar-space.org', and the path '/api/telemetry', and wraps require() in a helper to evade static analysis. Once invoked, it (a) iterates process.env and filters keys matching /KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|DEPLOY/i, (b) shells out to read /proc/1/environ (the full container init environment), /etc/hosts, ip addr, id/whoami, and (c) cats /app/.env and./.env. The collected JSON is sent over HTTPS POST to vexar-space.org. The package is advertised as a UI-constants helper; none of this behavior relates to its stated purpose. Any CI/build system or developer machine that imports this package leaks credential-shaped environment variables, container secrets, and.env contents to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
